cards.title: Think your parent is neurodivergent?
cards.body: Up to 97% of autistic people over 60 remain undiagnosed. Experts offer advice for family members on supporting this neglected generation.

cards.title: Managing anxiety on family holidays
cards.body: Constant worry can cloud family time, but learning to cope with real emergencies builds confidence. One parent discovered she was stronger than she thought when crisis struck.

cards.title: Childhood trauma shapes adult relationships
cards.body: Parents with difficult childhoods—narcissistic or avoidant—may struggle to recognize healthy relationships. Understanding these patterns helps break dysfunctional cycles.

cards.title: Only children aren't weird
cards.body: Being an only child or raising one doesn't produce selfish, lonely maladjusted kids. This growing demographic challenges persistent stereotypes about single-child families.

cards.title: Supporting bright children without tutors
cards.body: Grammar school entry needn't require expensive private tutoring. Parents should focus on their child's individual needs rather than projecting their own school anxieties.

cards.title: Pathological Demand Avoidance isn't permanent
cards.body: Families and proper treatment can gently reshape and improve PDA-linked patterns in children. With the right support, these behavioral challenges become manageable.
